properties.remark: Aircraft reported seeing waterspout associated with thunderstorm over the local Atlantic waters, about 26 miles east of Miami.
Multiple phone calls and social media reports of a tornado near Federal Hwy moving northeast towards Las Olas Blvd. Time is estimated.
Florida Highway Patrol reported that strong winds blew a semi-tractor trailer onto another vehicle on Krome Avenue south of SW 104 Street. Time estimated from radar.
Florida Highway Patrol Dispatch reported an empty semi-truck that was blown over (and into another vehicle) as a severe thunderstorm moved through the area. Time of event estimated via KAMX radar.

NWS Storm Survey found a few snapped trees near SW 88th Street and SW 157th Avenue. All the trees were damaged in the same direction with no signs of rotation detected. Straight-line wind speed of 60-70 mph estimated based on the damage. Time estimated from radar.

Miami Dade Police/Fire Rescue reported that Miami-Dade Transit called in a report of a downed tree blocking the roadway causing traffic disruptions. It has since been cleared. Time of event was estimated via radar.

NWS Storm Survey found a swath of straight-line wind damage that began with the overturned semi-tractor trailer on Krome Ave/SW 104th St and cut through several areas of The Hammocks, Kendale Lakes, Westchester, and West Miami. Vegetation damage including medium to large downed branches and snapped trees indicating 60 to 70 mph wind. This point is close to the end of the damage swath with time estimated from radar.
University of Miami Health System mesonet site reported a 67 mph wind gust at 1120 AM with a line of severe thunderstorms moving through the area.
Mesonet station XGVT at Government Cut reported a 52 mph/45 kt wind gust at 1125 AM with a passing thunderstorm.

A brief tornado of unknown intensity touched down approximately 4 miles north of the intersection of SW 8th street and Highway 997 (Krome Avenue). Time estimated by radar and video reports from the public.
Public reported tree downed at the intersection of Taft street and N Flamingo Road.

Wind damage, with a possible brief tornado touchdown, reported near the intersection of Stirling Road and NW 101st avenue. A few benches were damaged, metal sliding debri and broken tree branches around the Cooper City Optimist Club park.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SW Cooper City. An expected brief tornado touched down, reported near the intersection of Stirling Road and NW 101st avenue. A few benches were damaged, metal sliding debris and broken tree branches around the Cooper City Optimist Club park.
Strong wind gusts from a thunderstorm caused a car to flip over near the intersection of NE 3rd Ave and NE 34th Ct in Oakland Park.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Oakland Park. An expected tornado caused a car to flip over near the intersection of NE 3rd Ave and NE 34th Ct in Oakland Park.
Video and photos showing hail between 1 and 1.5 inches in diameter.

FPL power crews reported several large tree branches and trees down on power lines resulted in power outages in the vicinity of Griffin Rd & Davie Rd Extension. FPL meteorologist provided photos of damage via email. Time of damage estimated on KAMX radar as a severe warned cell moved over the area.
Earth Networks mesonet CLWCH Seminole Tribe Headquarters recorded a gust of 64 mph at 4:39 pm as a severe thunderstorm moved through the area. Site elevation is listed as 13 feet.
City of Hollywood reports numerous trees down in the vicinity of Sheridan St and the Florida Turnpike. Time estimated from radar.
Member of the public reports quarter sized hail in East Davie.
ASOS station KHWO Pembroke Pines reported wind gust of 50 kts (58 mph). Station elevation 7 ft.
Member of the public shared two videos of nickel-to-quarter sized hail falling as a strong thunderstorm moved through the area.
Local media reported a large tree down along Campo Sano Avenue in Coral Gables due to a strong thunderstorm wind gust.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20 reported a 41 mph wind gust at 719 PM.
Off duty NWS employee sent a video of rushing water down Lenox Ave and 16th St.
Law enforcement reported via social media flash flooding on Southern Blvd near Washington Rd. 4 cars are stalled out in high waters.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20 reported a wind gust of 41 mph as a gusty thunderstorm moved through the area.
Trained spotter called reporting flooded roads at 137 Ave and Eureka Dr.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 ENE Immokalee. Several downed trees and powerlines across Immokalee. Tree down at South 9th and Palmetto Ave blocking entire roadway.
Several downed trees and powerlines across Immokalee. Tree down at 59th and Palmetto Ave blocking entire roadway.
Pictures on social media of damage left behind after strong thunderstorm. Medium-large tree limbs broken, power line poles down, tents blown over.
Social media report of quarter sized hail near Lyons Road and Gulfstream Preserve as a severe thunderstorm moved through the area. Time estimated via radar.
Photos of quarter-sized hail at intersection of Lantana and Jog Roads.
Marine thunderstorm wind of 55 mph or 48 kt was recorded by XBOY Boynton Beach Mesonet.

Social media report of several large tree branches and snapped trees as a severe thunderstorm moved through the area. Location is just south of where a weather station reported a wind gust of 55mph wind gust. Social media report also indicated that there was a good bit of similar tree damage within a mile of the LSR location. Time is estimated via radar.
WXFlow station Hillsboro Light (XHBI).
Mesonet station XJUP Juno Beach Pier.
Off duty NWS Employee reports quarter size hail near Palm Beach Gardens.
Public reports, via broadcast media, of downed trees and powerlines due to winds at SW 202 Ave & Pines Blvd.
ASOS FXE reports gust of 67 mph at 2028Z.
ASOS FXE METAR 1/2SM +TSRA.
Lake Okeechobee West End Wind 25G53mph.
City of Cooper City reports tree down that is blocking Stonebridge Parkway near Court View Drive.
Public reported ping pong ball size hail (1.5 inches) from the thunderstorm.
Public reported uprooted tree in the intersection.
Public reported Palm Tree in the middle of the road.
Public sent in video showing quarter size hail falling from thunderstorm in the Pinecrest, FL. area.
Power pole down from thunderstorm wind gusts.

Social media footage of straight-line winds resulting from the downburst of a severe thunderstorm. Winds are estimated via the video to be in the 50-60mph range. Time was given based on the timestamp of the video footage.
Street flooding ongoing at NE 1ST AVE and 29th ST in Miami, FL. standing water up to 6 inches (half a foot) deep.
Media relayed video of up to half-dollar sized hail. Time estimate via radar. Delayed report via social media.
Mesonet station Fswn Miami Dade College Homestead reported a 58 mph gust. Emergency manager forwarded some pictures of a couple large branches snapped off of a tree.
Rockport Ct WeatherFlow station reported a 67 mph wind gust on Marco Island at 525 PM related to a severe thunderstorm in the area.
Cristobal St WeatherFlow station reported a 56 mph wind gust on Marco Island at 525 PM related to a severe thunderstorm in the area.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 1 SE Marco Island. Lamplighter Ct WeatherFlow station reported a 59 mph wind gust on Marco Island at 550 PM related to a severe thunderstorm in the area.
Lamplighter Ct WeatherFlow station reported a 59 mph wind gust on Marco Island at 550 PM related to a severe thunderstorm in the area.
Local broadcast media reported Marco Island residents received damage from strong winds. Report and pictures of flag poles snapped and laying on the ground in two yards.
Report of flooding on Forest Hill Blvd in West Palm Beach near Forest Hill Community High School. Approximately 1 inch of rain fell in 30 minutes in portions of WPB.

Corrects time and location of previous landspout report from 7 SSW Moore Haven. Hendry County Emergency Management confirmed reports of a brief landspout south of State Road 80 near Lexington Parkway. Fire Rescue personnel who observed the landspout stated that it traveled over citrus/sugar fields. This occurred as a severe thunderstorm was just to the west of this feature. No additional damage has been reported at this time. Broadcast Media also reported photo and video of the landspout.

Hendry County Emergency Management confirmed reports of a brief landspout near County Road 833 just south of the intersection of State Road 80. Fire Rescue who observed the landspout stated that it traveled over citrus/sugar fields. This occurred as a severe thunderstorm was just to the west of this feature. No additional damage has been reported at this time. Broadcast Media also reported photo and video of the landspout on the ground.
64 mph gust at Opa Locka Airport (KOPF).
58 mph gust at Hollywood North Perry Airport (HWO).
A snap tree reported by a member of the public.
Public reported tree down.
40 kt gust at Lake Oke West End.
48 kt wind gust at Lake Oke North Site.
Report relayed via pilot of a waterspout associated with a shower east of Miami Beach.
Juno Beach Pier measured a wind gust of 38 kts or 44 mph.
Significant ponding seen on the roads at the intersection of SW 1st Ave and 10th St.
Street flooding in downtown Miami at Biscayne Blvd and NE 14th Steet. Picture on social media shows approximately 3 to 4 inches of water on the roads.
City of Hallandale Beach social media post mentions blocked roads (NE 8th Ave and East Hallandale Beach Blvd) due to heavy rainfall and flooded roadways.
City of Hallandale Beach social media post mentioned blocked roads (NE 8th Ave and East Hallandale Beach Blvd) due to heavy rainfall and flooded roadways.

Public reported flooded streets and sidewalks in the area of Byron Ave and 69th Street in Miami Beach. Photographs showed water nearly reaching into the front doors of several homes. Report also included flooded cars in the area.
Tree Down at NW 59th AVE & Buchanan street along with a house driveway carport damage.
Mesonet station XGVT Government Cut reported a wind gust of 55mph as a strong thunderstorm moved offshore.
Sterling Road between SW 2nd and SW 3rd Ave in Dania Beach - under water.
Pictures on social media from Bay Harbor Islands shows water on flooded roadways up to bottom of the cars and covering sidewalks.
Water on flooded roadways approximately 6 to 12 inches. Stalled cars and water up to bus stop bench seat.
Flooded roadways at Dixie Hwy and Atlantic Blvd. Water on roads creating conditions not drivable.
Social media video shows flood roadways in Hallandale Beach near Federal Hwy and East Pembroke Rd. The water is approaching the bottom of car windows of a sedan.
Reports of standing water on airport runways at Ft. Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport. There are delays, cancellations, & standing water on the neighboring roadways.
Social media post shows flooded roadways in Hallandale at E Hallandale Beach Blvd and Hwy 1. Water is up to bottom of small cars, covering sidewalks and road medians.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 2 NW Fort Lauderdale. Interstate 95 southbound traffic is being diverted onto Oakland Park Blvd. The entrances to Interstate 95 at Sunrise Blvd, Broward Blvd, Davie Blvd, and SR-84 have been closed due to flooding and inaccessible roadways.
Interstate 95 southbound traffic is being diverted onto Oakland Park Blvd. The entrances to Interstate 95 at Sunrise Blvd, Broward Blvd, Davie Blvd, and SR-84 have been closed.
Miami-Dade EM reports that the fire department made a rescue from a home due to 3 - 4 feet of water in home. In a neighborhood near NW 145th St and NW 7th Ave in North Miami.

95 ramp south bound at 151ST flooded. 95 ramp North bound at 135ST flooded. 95 South Express lanes flooded at NW 67th ST. 95 North/South flooded at US 441. Gratigny Parkway flooded at NW 42ST and at NW 32st.
Social media pictures and videos of flooding and approximately 12 to 18 inches of water on the ground at Morningside Park.
Social media pictures show flooding in North Bay Village. Water on the roads is up to the bottom of cars.
Fort Lauderdale Airport FFA reported flooding on North ramp, control exits, west side of airport, and runway exits.
City of Weston reports flooding at 5:30PM. Weston Road & Emerald Estates Drive is not passable on Emerald Estates Dr.
Broadcast media reports street flooding on portions of Marco Island. Social media pictures show an estimate 3 to 5 inches on the roads.
Biscayne Bay Light Mesonet measured marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 kts or 46 mph. Elevated of measurement is 20 ft.

Florida Power & Light Met. passed along photos taken by field crew of a few wooden powerlines bent and felled over as a strong storm moved through the area. Estimation of velocities on radar, nearby observations, and analysis of the downed powerlines indicate maximum winds of near 50-55 mph occurred as the downburst reached the surface.
Lake Oke North End Sensor measured gust to 44 kts (51 mph). Elevation 13 ft.
Two reports from the public of a waterspout seen off the coast of Marco Island.
Trained storm spotter reported a waterspout associated with a thunderstorm approximately 11 nautical miles east of Key Biscayne. Picture confirmation was provided via social media.
Public reported minor tree damage and fence damage near Pembroke Road associated with a strong thunderstorm.
Pictures shared by an employee on social media showing waterspout off Marco Island coast.
Report from Hendry County emergency manager of minor damage to a metal roof in Montura, as well as minor damage to a nearby metal fence.
Mesonet station XJUP Juno Beach Pier measured a 43 MPH/37 KTS gusts with a strong thunderstorm.
Mesonet station XGVT Government Cut measured 45 MPH/39 KTS with a passing rain storm.
Pilot reported a waterspout about 3 miles offshore associated with a small thunderstorm around 7 AM EDT.
Mesonet station XTKY Turkey Point reported a wind gust of 43mph at 4:09pm.
Mesonet station XTKY Turkey Point reported a wind gust of 50mph at 4:34pm.
Mesonet station XTKY Turkey Point reported a wind gust of 53 mph at 5:29pm.
Mesonet station XJUP Juno Beach Pier reported a wind gust of 59mph at 6:06pm.
Mesonet station 1978W Fswn Glades Harney Pond Park reported a wind gust of 47mph at 7:00pm.
Mesonet station XTKY Turkey Point reported a wind gust of 53mph at 7:54pm.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20 measured wind gust of 45 kt or 52 mph.
Mesonet station XPEG Port Everglades measured gust of 41 kts or 47 mph.
Mesonet station XDAN Dania Pier measured wind gust of 63 kts or 73 mph.
Mesonet station XPEG Port Everglades measured 73 mph wind gust.
Water in multiple homes on Grenada Blvd.
Water in garages of homes along Cooper Dr.
50-70 stranded vehicles (estimate) and flood water intrusion into some low-lying buildings and coastal structures.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20. Elevation 20 ft.
Buoy station VAKF1 Virginia Key, FL.
Charter Club of Marco Beach reported a wind gust of 51 mph or 44 kts.
Mesonet station XJUP at Juno Beach Pier reported a 44 mph/38 kt wind gust at 326 PM from a thunderstorm.
Mesonet station XJUP at Juno Beach Pier reported a 47 mph/41 kt wind gust at 331 PM with a thunderstorm.
Off-duty NWS Meteorologist reported waterspout sighting approximately 4 miles east-northeast of Deerfield Beach.
Local broadcast media reports seeing waterspout off the coast near Ft. Lauderdale.
Mesonet station XKBS Biscayne Bay Light 20 reported a wind gust of 50mph at 4:22pm as a strong thunderstorm moved offshore.
Turkey Point mesonet recorded a wind gust of 39 kts or 45 mph.
FXE ASOS reported waterspout at 14:36 local time.
39 Kt gust at Lake Worth (LKWF1) maritime observation site.
Multiple reports of twin waterspouts east of Manatee Lagoon associated with a large thunderstorm complex, starting at 1:45PM and continuing until at least 2:15PM.
Waterspout reported east of Deerfield Beach around 8 AM. Report relayed via social media.
Broadcast media relayed a public report via social media of a waterspout offshore of Naples around 342 PM this afternoon.
A report from social media showed a waterspout off the coast of Miami Beach associated with a shower in the area. Time estimated from radar.
Member of the public reported a waterspout sighted out over the offshore Palm Beach county waters.

Social media post shows flooded road, at Collins Ave and 23rd St, up to doors at local convenience store. People walking through waters on road almost up their knees. City of Miami tweet showed two pumps set up at NE 23rd St.
Broadcast media relayed a report of a waterspout off Naples at 952 AM via social media.
44mph gust at SFWMD Lake Oke Center.
Hillsboro Inlet mesonet reported a gust of 38 kt or 44 mph.
Port Everglades measured a gust of 37 kt or 43 mph.
Powerlines down across Military and Vilma roads.
ASOS station KFLL Fort Lauderdale/hollywood Intl Airport.
Significant Standing water of 3 to 6 inches at 1801 Purdy Ave.
Significant Standing Water 3 to 6 inches at 1975 71ST street.
First report of possible tornado. Photos of fence and small tree damage as well. Pending damage photo review.
Corrects previous tornado report from 2 W Fortymile Bend. Multiple snapped hardwood limbs and mangroves due to a tornado. This tornado report is new after storm surveys and post-event analysis.
Multiple snapped hardwood limbs and mangroves due to a tornado. This tornado report is new after storm surveys and post-event analysis.

Preliminary starting point for a confirmed tornado that crossed I-75 near the Miccosukee Service Station and moved north to the Brighton Reservation. Pending survey will determine details about continuity of track.
Preliminary starting point for a confirmed tornado that moved across I-75, SR-27, and east of Belle Glade and Pahokee. Pending survey will detail the continuity of the track.
Tornado was observed south of US98, briefly lifted, and returned just north of US98. It continued for 10 minutes as it went north. This is a new tornado report based on post-event analysis.
Preliminary starting point for a confirmed tornado that moved from Hendry into Glades county crossing SR 80. Pending storm survey.
Beginning time/location of the EF1 Lakeport Tornado via KAMX and NWS Storm Survey.
Preliminary starting point for a confirmed tornado that formed near the Hendry and Glades county line, crossing US 27 and moving into the Brighton Reservation. Pending survey.
NWS Storm Survey found small limbs broken by a tornadic circulation. This is a new tornado report.
Bent utility pole near agricultural facility. This is a new tornado report assessed by a NWS Storm Survey.
Trees snapped across Sam Jones Trail from FPL solar farm. This is a new tornado report after a NWS Storm Survey.
Start point of the EF3 Lakeport Tornado. Time estimated off of KAMX.

Corrects previous tornado report from 7 WNW Lion Country Safari Park. Corrects previous tornado report from 7 WNW Lion Country Safari Park. Spotter witnessed tornado touchdown looking north from 441/SR-80. This is a new tornado report after post-event analysis.
Corrects previous tornado report from 7 WNW Lion Country Safari Park. Spotter witnessed tornado touchdown looking north from 441/SR-80. This is a new tornado report after post-event analysis.
Spotter witnessed tornado touchdown looking north from 441/SR-80. This is a new tornado report after post-event analysis.
AWOS station KAPF 1 ENE Naples reported a wind gust of 58mph as a band of Hurricane Milton moved through the area.
Tornadic waterspout captured on video over Lake Okeechobee. Touchdown occurred after cell moved over water and no damage was observed where it exited the Lake near Okeechobee around 3:45 PM.
Mesonet station PEGF1 2 NE Dania Beach reported a wind gust of 49mph.
Mesonet station XJUP Juno Beach Pier reported a wind gust of 48 mph.
Corrects previous tornado report from 4 W Lion Country Safari Park. Two different spotter reports of a tornado on the ground. Time corrected based on storm survey findings.
Two different spotter reports of a tornado on the ground. Pending storm survey.

First video evidence of confirmed tornado near Southern Blvd that will pass west of Palm Beach Gardens and cross Bee Line Highway before crossing the Palm Beach County line after Jupiter Farms. Pending survey.
Mesonet station KF45 North Palm Beach County General reported a wind gust of 92mph as a confirmed tornado on the ground passed over or very near the airport.
Waterspout seen off Boynton Inlet reported by lifeguard at 10 am.
